title: Squiz Plan Index
description: Top-level descriptor at plan/index.json. Declares metadata + which sections this plan includes. Section data lives in sibling files (plan/overview.json, plan/functional.json, plan/non-functional.json, plan/cases.json, plan/engineering.json, plan/build.json, plus any custom sections). Each sibling section file is a SectionFile (see $defs.sectionFile) — not validated by this schema since it covers only index.json.

title.description: Plan H1 — the headline shown above the tab strip.

theme.description: Optional theme name; same precedence as squiz. One of: paper, phosphor, amber, beige, rose, ocean, forest, slate. Empty/omitted lets the renderer's per-repo auto-rotation pick one.

sections.description: Section IDs to load + render order. Canonical IDs (overview, functional, non-functional, cases, engineering, build) sort to their canonical position; unknown IDs append. Each ID NAME corresponds to a sibling file <NAME>.json next to this index.

sectionFile.description: Per-section payload at plan/<sectionId>.json. NOT validated by this schema; described here for reference only.

item.description: One entry in a section. ID must use the section's prefix (validated by the parser). Refs are IDs of parent items the validator confirms exist. When Options is non-empty the item is a *decision* — the renderer shows a chooser (same shape as squiz options) and the user's pick is captured in the feedback export as `chose: "optionId"`. When Options is empty (the v0.3.0 default) the item is a *statement* — flat card, no chooser.

id.description: Full item ID with section prefix (e.g. OVR-1, FR-3.2, BUILD-cli-flags).

art.description: Same forms as squiz Option.art (wf:/arch:/DSL/raw SVG/"none"/omitted).

refs.description: IDs of parent items (validator checks they exist).

options.description: v0.4.0: optional in-item chooser. When non-empty, the item is a decision; when empty/omitted, it's a statement.

option.description: One branch of an in-item decision. Same shape as squiz's Option (id/label/name/desc/art) so authors can copy patterns between the two tools. IDs are local to the item — collisions across items are fine; collisions within one item are not (validated).

id.description: Stable slug, comes back in feedback as `chose`.

recommendation.description: When non-empty, marks this option as the author's recommended choice and carries the explanation. The renderer shows a "★ RECOMMENDED" chip + the explanation as a small editorial callout under the option's desc. At most one option per item should carry one; multiple renders all of them but is usually an authoring mistake.
